description: Comprehensive OBD-II Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) dataset targeting vehicles used in Pakistan. Covers generic SAE J2012 codes and manufacturer-specific codes for Toyota, Honda, Suzuki, Hyundai, Kia, Changan, Haval/GWM, MG, Daihatsu, and Nissan.

pakistan_specific_fault_patterns: Low-quality / low-octane petrol causing knock, injector, catalyst and O2 sensor damage
CNG kit conversions (extremely common) causing lean codes P0171, misfire P0300, and MAF sensor issues P0101
Dusty road conditions clogging MAF sensors (P0101 is #1 sensor fault in Pakistan)
Neglected engine oil changes causing VVT faults P0010/P0011, VTEC P1259, and bearing damage P1326
Extreme summer heat (45°C+) causing overheating, sensor failures, and ECM water ingress during monsoon
Rough roads (potholes) damaging wheel speed sensors C0010–C0040
Neglected ATF/CVT fluid causing transmission codes P0730, P0750, P0780
AGS (AMT) clutch issues P1652–P1655 very common in city traffic on Suzuki Alto/Cultus/WagonR
Old HV battery on imported Toyota Prius/Aqua (P0A80 very common)
Loose fuel cap EVAP codes P0455/P0457 very common
Immobilizer issues B2799 on imported JDM vehicles with ownership changes
market_context.jdm_import_note: A significant portion of Pakistan's used car market are JDM (Japanese Domestic Market) imports including Toyota Prius, Aqua, Vitz, Premio, Axio, Daihatsu Mira, Nissan Dayz – these often arrive with existing fault codes or age-related sensor failures. CVT fluid is often not changed on these imports.

code_type_legend.Generic: SAE J2012 standard OBD-II code – applies to all OBD-II compliant vehicles

vehicles.segment: Mid-size SUV – fastest growing in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Volume Regulator Control Circuit Open
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel volume regulator wiring for open circuit.
Inspect ECM fuel regulator output pin.
Check regulator solenoid resistance (2–5 Ω).
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Toyota Fortuner 2.8D, Hilux Revo 2.8D, Nissan Navara 2.5D

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Volume Regulator Control Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel pressure with scan tool.
Inspect high-pressure pump metering valve.
Check for fuel contamination.

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Volume Regulator Control Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check regulator circuit for short to ground.
Inspect wiring harness for chafing near exhaust.

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Volume Regulator Control Circuit High
diagnostic_procedure: Check regulator circuit for short to battery or open.
Test ECM driver output.

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit / Open Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check VVT solenoid wiring and connector.
Measure solenoid resistance (6.9–7.9 Ω typical).
Check oil level and quality – low oil is major VVT fault cause in Pakistan.
Perform VVT solenoid activation test with scan tool.
Inspect oil galleries for sludge blocking VVT flow.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Toyota Corolla 1.6/1.8, Yaris, Vitz, Prius, Honda City 1.5, Civic 1.5T, Hyundai Tucson, Kia Sportage

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Change engine oil if overdue – #1 cause in Pakistan.
Check VVT solenoid (OCV) for sticking or contamination.
Inspect timing chain stretch or worn tensioner.
Check camshaft position sensor signal.
Perform VVT actuator test.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Toyota Corolla, Yaris, Vitz, Honda City, Civic, Hyundai Tucson, Kia Sportage

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Retarded Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check VVT solenoid and oil condition.
Inspect timing chain for excessive wear.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Toyota Corolla, Honda City/Civic, Hyundai Tucson/Elantra

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust (B) Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit / Open Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check exhaust VVT solenoid wiring and resistance.
Inspect ECM exhaust cam control circuit.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Honda Civic 1.5T, Toyota Corolla 1.8 Dual VVT-i

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust (B) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check exhaust VVT solenoid for sticking.
Check oil condition and level.
Inspect timing chain system.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Honda Civic 1.5T, Toyota Corolla Altis, Hyundai Tucson, Kia Sportage

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust (B) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Retarded Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check exhaust cam VVT solenoid.
Check oil pressure and quality.

dtc_codes.description: Crankshaft Position – Camshaft Position Correlation Bank 1 Sensor A
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect timing chain for stretch.
Check crankshaft (CKP) and camshaft (CMP) sensor signals.
Verify timing chain tensioner operation.
Check if timing chain has been correctly set after service.
On Haval H6/Jolion: known timing chain wear issue – inspect tensioner.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All multi-cylinder petrol engines including Haval H6/Jolion 1.5T

dtc_codes.description: Crankshaft Position – Camshaft Position Correlation Bank 1 Sensor B
diagnostic_procedure: Check exhaust camshaft position vs. crankshaft.
Inspect timing chain system for stretch.
Check exhaust CMP sensor wiring.

dtc_codes.description: HO2S Heater Control Circuit Bank 1 Sensor 1 (Upstream)
diagnostic_procedure: Check upstream O2 sensor heater fuse.
Measure heater resistance (2–30 Ω when warm).
Check ECM heater PWM output.
Common in older high-mileage Pakistan cars – replace O2 sensor.

dtc_codes.description: HO2S Heater Control Circuit Low Bank 1 Sensor 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check O2 sensor heater circuit for short to ground.
Check heater relay if equipped.

dtc_codes.description: HO2S Heater Control Circuit High Bank 1 Sensor 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check O2 sensor heater circuit for short to battery.
Test ECM heater driver.

dtc_codes.description: HO2S Heater Control Circuit Bank 1 Sensor 2 (Downstream)
diagnostic_procedure: Check downstream O2 sensor heater circuit.
Measure heater resistance.
Check fuse for downstream heater circuit.

dtc_codes.description: HO2S Heater Control Circuit Low Bank 1 Sensor 2
diagnostic_procedure: Check downstream sensor heater for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: HO2S Heater Control Circuit High Bank 1 Sensor 2
diagnostic_procedure: Check downstream sensor heater for short to battery.

dtc_codes.description: MAP/MAF – Throttle Position Correlation
diagnostic_procedure: Check MAP sensor vacuum line for leaks.
Inspect MAF sensor for contamination (common in dusty Pakistan roads).
Clean MAF sensor with approved cleaner spray.
Check throttle body for carbon deposits.

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Rail/System Pressure Too Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel pump delivery pressure (min 3 bar GDI, 0.3 bar MPI).
Inspect fuel filter – may be clogged with low-quality Pakistan petrol.
Check fuel pump current draw.
Inspect HPFP and cam follower wear (GDI engines).
Check fuel injector leakdown.
Note: CNG kit conversions can starve fuel system – check CNG regulator.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All fuel-injected vehicles – common with CNG kits in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Rail/System Pressure Too High
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel pressure relief valve.
Inspect HPFP solenoid valve.
Check fuel pressure sensor accuracy.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Toyota Corolla GDI, Honda Civic 1.5T, Haval H6 1.5T

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Pressure Regulator 1 Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel pressure sensor and HPFP metering valve.
Inspect low-pressure fuel pump supply.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: GDI/TGDI engines: Honda Civic 1.5T, Haval H6 1.5T, Kia Stonic 1.4T

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Clean MAF sensor (very common fix in Pakistan due to dust).
Inspect air filter for excessive clogging.
Check MAF sensor wiring and connector.
Verify intake system for cracks or leaks.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All petrol vehicles – extremely common in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Clean MAF sensor hot wire element.
Check for air leaks between MAF and throttle body.
Inspect air filter – replace if heavily soiled.
On CNG-converted vehicles: check CNG mixer effect on MAF signal.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All petrol vehicles – #1 sensor fault in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Low Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check MAF circuit for short to ground or open.
Inspect sensor connector for moisture (common in monsoon season).
Measure 5V reference at MAF.

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit High Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check MAF circuit for short to voltage.
Test sensor output signal with multimeter.

dtc_codes.description: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check MAP sensor vacuum hose for cracks or blockage.
Measure sensor output at idle vs. WOT.
Inspect for intake manifold leaks.

dtc_codes.description: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor Circuit Low Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check MAP sensor circuit for short to ground.
Measure 5V supply at sensor.

dtc_codes.description: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor Circuit High Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check MAP sensor circuit for short to battery.
Inspect sensor for internal failure.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check IAT sensor (often integrated in MAF).
Compare IAT vs. ambient temperature at cold start.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check IAT circuit for short to ground.
Inspect MAF/IAT sensor connector.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit High
diagnostic_procedure: Check IAT circuit for open or short to voltage.
Inspect sensor element for damage.

dtc_codes.description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Verify thermostat is opening correctly.
Compare ECT vs. IAT at cold soak.
Check coolant level – low coolant causes erratic ECT.
Common with overheating issues in Pakistan summer.

dtc_codes.description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check ECT sensor circuit for short to ground.
Measure sensor resistance (2.5 kΩ at 20°C, 300 Ω at 80°C).

dtc_codes.description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit High Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check ECT sensor circuit for open or short to voltage.
Inspect sensor connector for corrosion.

dtc_codes.description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit Intermittent
diagnostic_procedure: Wiggle test ECT harness for intermittent connection.
Check connector terminal tension.

dtc_codes.description: Coolant Temperature Below Thermostat Regulating Temperature
diagnostic_procedure: Check thermostat – stuck open is common cause.
Verify ECT sensor accuracy.
Common in cold mornings in Islamabad/Lahore.

dtc_codes.description: Throttle Position Sensor/Switch A Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Perform throttle body adaptation/reset with scan tool.
Check TPS voltage sweep (0.3–4.7V).
Inspect throttle body for carbon deposits – common with low-quality fuel.
Check for sticking throttle plate.

dtc_codes.description: Throttle Position Sensor/Switch A Circuit Low Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check TPS circuit for short to ground.
Inspect throttle body connector.

dtc_codes.description: Throttle Position Sensor/Switch A Circuit High Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check TPS circuit for short to voltage or open.
Perform throttle body adaptation after repair.

dtc_codes.description: System Too Lean Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check for vacuum leaks – inspect all hoses and intake manifold gasket.
Clean MAF sensor.
Check fuel pressure.
Inspect O2 sensor for accuracy.
On CNG-converted vehicles (very common in Pakistan): check CNG mixture ratio, leaking gas mixer, or faulty CNG ECU causing lean condition when switching fuel modes.
Check fuel injectors for clogging.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All petrol vehicles – very common in Pakistan especially with CNG kits

dtc_codes.description: System Too Rich Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check for flooding injector or stuck-open injector.
Inspect fuel pressure regulator for failure.
Check MAF sensor for over-reading.
Inspect for coolant or oil entering combustion.

dtc_codes.description: System Too Lean Bank 2
diagnostic_procedure: Check Bank 2 for vacuum leaks.
Check Bank 2 injectors.
Check MAF sensor.

dtc_codes.description: System Too Rich Bank 2
diagnostic_procedure: Check Bank 2 for rich conditions.
Inspect injectors and fuel pressure.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction Bank 1 Sensor 1 (Upstream)
diagnostic_procedure: Check upstream O2 sensor wiring and connector.
Verify sensor is reaching operating temperature.
Check for exhaust leaks upstream of sensor.
Test sensor switching frequency at idle.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check for lean misfire or vacuum leaks.
Inspect sensor circuit for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check for rich mixture or oil burning.
Check fuel pressure.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response Bank 1 Sensor 1
diagnostic_procedure: Replace upstream O2 sensor – common in high-mileage Pakistan vehicles.
Check exhaust leaks near sensor.
Verify sensor heater operation.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Bank 1 Sensor 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check sensor heater circuit.
Inspect wiring for open circuit.
Replace O2 sensor if dead.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Heater Circuit Malfunction Bank 1 Sensor 1
diagnostic_procedure: Measure O2 sensor heater resistance.
Check heater fuse and relay.
Check ECM heater control output.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction Bank 1 Sensor 2 (Downstream)
diagnostic_procedure: Check downstream O2 sensor and wiring.
Verify catalytic converter is not destroyed.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 2
diagnostic_procedure: Check downstream sensor circuit for short to ground.
Inspect wiring near hot exhaust pipe.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 2
diagnostic_procedure: Check downstream sensor for contamination.
Inspect catalytic converter efficiency.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response Bank 1 Sensor 2
diagnostic_procedure: Replace downstream O2 sensor.
Check catalytic converter for damage.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Bank 1 Sensor 2
diagnostic_procedure: Check downstream sensor wiring for open circuit.
Replace sensor if failed.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Heater Circuit Malfunction Bank 1 Sensor 2
diagnostic_procedure: Measure downstream O2 heater resistance.
Check heater supply and ECM control circuit.

dtc_codes.description: Injector Circuit / Open – Cylinder 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 1 resistance (11–17 Ω MPI, 0.3–1.5 Ω GDI).
Inspect injector connector and wiring.
Swap injector with known-good unit to confirm.

dtc_codes.description: Injector Circuit / Open – Cylinder 2
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 2 resistance and wiring.
Swap with known-good injector.

dtc_codes.description: Injector Circuit / Open – Cylinder 3
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 3 resistance and wiring.

dtc_codes.description: Injector Circuit / Open – Cylinder 4
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 4 resistance and wiring.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 1 Injector Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 1 for short to ground.
Inspect ECM injector driver output.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 1 Injector Circuit High
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 1 for short to battery.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 1 Contribution/Balance Fault
diagnostic_procedure: Perform injector balance test.
Inspect compression on cylinder 1.
Check for air leak at cylinder 1 intake port.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 2 Injector Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 2 for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 2 Injector Circuit High
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 2 for short to battery.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 2 Contribution/Balance Fault
diagnostic_procedure: Perform injector balance test on cylinder 2.
Check compression.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 3 Injector Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 3 for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 3 Injector Circuit High
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 3 for short to battery.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 4 Injector Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 4 for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 4 Injector Circuit High
diagnostic_procedure: Check injector 4 for short to battery.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 4 Contribution/Balance Fault
diagnostic_procedure: Perform injector balance test on cylinder 4.
Check compression.

dtc_codes.description: Random/Multiple Cylinder Misfire Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Replace spark plugs – use OEM, not cheap Chinese copies.
Inspect ignition coils – swap to isolate.
Check fuel injectors for clogging.
Check compression on all cylinders.
Inspect timing chain/belt condition.
On CNG-converted vehicles: check CNG injectors, spark plug gap (use tighter gap for CNG), and gas pressure regulator.
Note: Most common check-engine light code in Pakistan.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All petrol vehicles – #1 most common code in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 1 Misfire Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Check/replace Cylinder 1 spark plug.
Swap ignition coil 1 to another cylinder to diagnose.
Check injector 1 spray pattern.
Perform compression test on cylinder 1.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 2 Misfire Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Check/replace Cylinder 2 spark plug.
Swap ignition coil 2 to diagnose.
Check injector 2.
Perform compression test on cylinder 2.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 3 Misfire Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Check/replace Cylinder 3 spark plug.
Swap ignition coil 3 to diagnose.
Check injector 3.
Perform compression test on cylinder 3.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 4 Misfire Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Check/replace Cylinder 4 spark plug.
Swap ignition coil 4 to diagnose.
Check injector 4.
Perform compression test on cylinder 4.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 5 Misfire Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Check Cylinder 5 spark plug and coil.
Test compression.

dtc_codes.description: Cylinder 6 Misfire Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Check Cylinder 6 spark plug and coil.
Test compression.

dtc_codes.description: Ignition/Distributor Engine Speed Input Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check crankshaft position sensor (CKP) and tone wheel.
Inspect air gap between sensor and ring gear.

dtc_codes.description: Ignition/Distributor Engine Speed Input Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check CKP sensor and tone wheel for damage.
Inspect sensor air gap (0.5–1.5 mm).

dtc_codes.description: Ignition/Distributor Engine Speed Input Circuit No Signal
diagnostic_procedure: Check CKP sensor wiring for open circuit.
Test sensor supply voltage.
Check for metal debris on sensor tip.

dtc_codes.description: Knock Control System Error
diagnostic_procedure: Check knock sensor wiring and mounting torque.
Note: Low-octane petrol in Pakistan causes genuine knock – use premium fuel.

dtc_codes.description: Knock Sensor 1 Circuit Malfunction Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check knock sensor wiring and connector.
Measure sensor resistance.
Check ECM knock detection circuit.
Low-octane petrol in Pakistan can cause genuine knock.

dtc_codes.description: Knock Sensor 1 Circuit Low Input Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check knock sensor circuit for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: Knock Sensor 1 Circuit High Input Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check knock sensor circuit for short to battery.

dtc_codes.description: Camshaft Position Sensor Circuit Malfunction Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check CMP sensor wiring and connector.
Test sensor power supply (5V) and ground.
Measure sensor air gap.
Replace sensor if no output.

dtc_codes.description: Camshaft Position Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check CMP sensor trigger wheel for damage.
Compare CMP vs. CKP signal timing.

dtc_codes.description: Camshaft Position Sensor Circuit Low Input Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check CMP sensor circuit for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: Camshaft Position Sensor Circuit High Input Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check CMP sensor circuit for short to voltage or open.

dtc_codes.description: Ignition Coil A Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check ignition coil 1 primary resistance (0.3–1.5 Ω).
Swap coil to another cylinder to confirm fault.

dtc_codes.description: Ignition Coil B Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check ignition coil 2. Swap to confirm.

dtc_codes.description: Ignition Coil C Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check ignition coil 3. Swap to confirm.

dtc_codes.description: Ignition Coil D Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check ignition coil 4. Swap to confirm.

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust Gas Recirculation Flow Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check EGR valve operation and wiring.
Inspect EGR passages for carbon clogging – very common in Pakistan with low-quality fuel.
Clean EGR valve with EGR cleaner.

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust Gas Recirculation Insufficient Flow Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Clean EGR valve and passages – typically heavily carbon-clogged in Pakistan.
Check EGR control solenoid.

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust Gas Recirculation Excessive Flow Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Check EGR valve for sticking in open position.
Inspect EGR solenoid.

dtc_codes.description: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check for misfires causing catalyst damage.
Verify O2 sensor accuracy upstream and downstream.
Inspect catalytic converter for physical damage.
Note: Low-quality fuel and leaded fuel additives in Pakistan can contaminate catalyst.
Replace catalytic converter if efficiency confirmed low.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All petrol vehicles – common >100,000 km in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold Bank 2
diagnostic_procedure: Same as P0420 for Bank 2.

dtc_codes.description: Evaporative Emission Control System Incorrect Purge Flow
diagnostic_procedure: Check EVAP purge valve for proper opening.
Inspect EVAP purge vacuum hose.

dtc_codes.description: Evaporative Emission Control System Leak Detected (Small)
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel cap seal – replace if cracked (common in Pakistan heat).
Perform smoke test on EVAP system.
Check EVAP hoses and canister.

dtc_codes.description: Evaporative Emission Control System Purge Control Valve Circuit
diagnostic_procedure: Check EVAP purge valve wiring and solenoid resistance.

dtc_codes.description: Evaporative Emission Control System Large Leak Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel cap – loose or damaged cap is #1 cause in Pakistan.
Inspect EVAP lines and canister.
Perform EVAP smoke test.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All petrol vehicles – very common in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Evaporative Emission Control System Very Small Leak Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect fuel cap seal.
Perform precision EVAP smoke test.

dtc_codes.description: EVAP Leak Detected – Fuel Cap Loose or Off
diagnostic_procedure: Tighten or replace fuel cap – almost always the cause.
Verify cap clicks 3 times when tightened.

dtc_codes.description: Cooling Fan 1 Control Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check cooling fan motor and relay.
Verify ECM fan control output.
Critical in Pakistan summers (45°C+) – overheating risk without fan.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All vehicles with electric cooling fan

dtc_codes.description: Idle Air Control System RPM Too Low
diagnostic_procedure: Clean throttle body – carbon deposits on IAC passage very common in Pakistan.
Perform throttle body adaptation.
Check for vacuum leaks at idle.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All petrol vehicles – very common in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Idle Air Control System RPM Too High
diagnostic_procedure: Check for vacuum leaks causing uncontrolled air.
Inspect idle air control valve for sticking open.
Check throttle blade for sticking.

dtc_codes.description: System Voltage Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check battery voltage (13.5–14.5V running).
Inspect alternator output.
Check ECM main fuse and relay.
Inspect battery terminals for corrosion – common in Pakistan heat.

dtc_codes.description: System Voltage Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check battery condition and charge.
Test alternator charging voltage.
Check for high resistance in charging circuit.

dtc_codes.description: System Voltage High
diagnostic_procedure: Check alternator voltage regulator.
Inspect for overcharging condition >15V.

dtc_codes.description: Serial Communication Link Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check OBD-II DLC communication pins.
Inspect CAN bus wiring for short or open.

dtc_codes.description: Internal Control Module Memory Check Sum Error
diagnostic_procedure: Reflash ECM with latest firmware.
Check ECM power supply and ground connections.
Replace ECM if reflash fails.

dtc_codes.description: Internal Control Module RAM Error
diagnostic_procedure: Reflash ECM.
Replace ECM if fault persists.

dtc_codes.description: Internal Control Module ROM Error
diagnostic_procedure: Reflash ECM.
Replace ECM if fault persists.

dtc_codes.description: ECM/PCM Processor Fault
diagnostic_procedure: Check ECM power supply and ground.
Check for ECM water ingress (common during Pakistan monsoon flooding).
Reflash or replace ECM.

dtc_codes.description: Throttle Actuator Control Range/Performance Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check throttle body motor and wiring.
Perform throttle body adaptation.
Clean throttle body if carbon-bound.

dtc_codes.description: Sensor Reference Voltage 'A' Circuit Open
diagnostic_procedure: Check 5V reference circuit for open.
Disconnect sensors one by one to find the one pulling down the reference.

dtc_codes.description: Sensor Reference Voltage 'A' Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check 5V reference circuit for short to ground.
Disconnect sensors to isolate short.

dtc_codes.description: Sensor Reference Voltage 'B' Circuit Open
diagnostic_procedure: Check 5V reference B circuit for open.

dtc_codes.description: Sensor Reference Voltage 'B' Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check 5V reference B for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: Transmission Control System Malfunction (TCM DTC Present)
diagnostic_procedure: Scan TCM for specific transmission codes.
Check transmission fluid level and condition – dark/burnt fluid very common in Pakistan.
Inspect transmission wiring harness.

dtc_codes.description: Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check transmission range sensor and wiring.
Verify shifter mechanism engagement.

dtc_codes.description: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check ATF temperature sensor.
Inspect transmission fluid level.

dtc_codes.description: Incorrect Gear Ratio Detected
diagnostic_procedure: Change ATF/CVT fluid – typically dark/burnt in Pakistan market.
Check transmission solenoids.
Inspect for mechanical transmission wear.
This is one of the most common AT faults in Pakistan due to neglected fluid service.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All AT/CVT vehicles – extremely common in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Gear 1 Incorrect Ratio
diagnostic_procedure: Check transmission fluid and solenoids.
Inspect 1st gear clutch pack.

dtc_codes.description: Gear 2 Incorrect Ratio
diagnostic_procedure: Check transmission fluid and solenoids.

dtc_codes.description: Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check TCC solenoid and wiring.
Inspect transmission fluid condition.

dtc_codes.description: Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Performance or Stuck Off
diagnostic_procedure: Check TCC solenoid valve for sticking.
Change ATF if degraded.
Change CVT fluid for Daihatsu/Nissan imports – often neglected.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All AT/CVT vehicles – also Daihatsu Mira, Nissan Sunny imports

dtc_codes.description: Shift Solenoid 'A' Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check shift solenoid A resistance (20–30 Ω typical).
Change ATF if contaminated.
Replace solenoid if stuck.

dtc_codes.description: Shift Solenoid 'B' Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check shift solenoid B and wiring.
Change ATF if needed.

dtc_codes.description: Shift Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Perform transmission fluid service.
Scan for specific solenoid codes.

dtc_codes.description: Park/Neutral Position Switch Input Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check PNP/inhibitor switch and wiring.
Adjust or replace switch.

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger/Supercharger A Overboost Condition
diagnostic_procedure: Check wastegate solenoid valve.
Inspect boost control hoses.
Verify boost pressure sensor accuracy.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Honda Civic 1.5T, Haval H6 1.5T, Jolion 1.5T, Kia Stonic 1.4T, Changan Oshan X7 1.5T, MG HS 1.5T

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger Boost Sensor A Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check boost pressure sensor wiring.
Measure 5V supply and ground.
Replace sensor if faulty.

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger Boost Sensor A Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check boost sensor accuracy.
Inspect intake for leaks.

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger Wastegate Solenoid A Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check wastegate solenoid resistance and wiring.
Test solenoid operation with scan tool.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Honda Civic 1.5T, Haval H6, Kia Stonic, MG HS

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger/Supercharger A Underboost Condition
diagnostic_procedure: Check for boost leaks in intercooler piping – very common in Pakistan due to heat cycling.
Check wastegate for sticking open.
Inspect turbocharger vanes for damage.
Verify air filter is not severely clogged.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Honda Civic 1.5T, Haval H6/Jolion 1.5T, Kia Stonic, Changan Oshan X7, MG HS

dtc_codes.description: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check LF wheel speed sensor wiring and connector.
Measure sensor resistance and air gap.
Inspect tone ring for damage or missing teeth.
Note: Common on rough Pakistani roads due to sensor damage from potholes.

dtc_codes.description: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check RF wheel speed sensor and wiring.
Inspect tone ring.

dtc_codes.description: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check LR wheel speed sensor.
Inspect tone ring.
Check for wiring damage near rear axle.

dtc_codes.description: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check RR wheel speed sensor.
Inspect tone ring and wiring.

dtc_codes.description: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check RR sensor output.
Inspect tone ring.

dtc_codes.description: ABS Motor Relay Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check ABS pump motor relay and fuse.
Test ABS motor operation.

dtc_codes.description: ABS/TCS Brake Switch Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check brake light switch and wiring.
Adjust or replace brake switch.

dtc_codes.description: Engine Control System Malfunction (ABS Inhibit)
diagnostic_procedure: Diagnose engine system first.
Check communication between ECM and ABS module.

dtc_codes.description: PATS Key Not Programmed / Non-Transponder Key Used
diagnostic_procedure: Program key using scan tool with immobilizer function.
Verify correct key type.
Check transponder antenna ring wiring.
Note: Key cloning is common in Pakistan – use genuine keys only.

dtc_codes.description: Engine Immobilizer System Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Re-register key with immobilizer ECU.
Check immobilizer antenna and receiver.
Very common issue with second-hand/imported JDM vehicles in Pakistan with ownership changes.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: All vehicles – especially imported JDM vehicles

dtc_codes.description: High Speed CAN Communication Bus – No Feedback
diagnostic_procedure: Check CAN bus wiring – measure resistance between CAN-H and CAN-L (~60 Ω with ECM unplugged).
Inspect all module connectors on CAN bus.
Check for broken, shorted, or corroded CAN wires.
Measure battery voltage (low voltage can cause CAN faults).

dtc_codes.description: Control Module Communication Bus 'A' Off
diagnostic_procedure: Check CAN-H and CAN-L wiring.
Measure bus resistance at DLC.
Locate short in CAN bus.

dtc_codes.description: Lost Communication with ECM/PCM 'A'
diagnostic_procedure: Check ECM power supply and ground.
Inspect CAN bus wiring from ECM.
Verify ECM is powered.

dtc_codes.description: Lost Communication with TCM
diagnostic_procedure: Check TCM power supply and CAN wiring.
Inspect TCM connector for corrosion.

dtc_codes.description: Lost Communication with ABS Control Module
diagnostic_procedure: Check ABS module power supply.
Inspect CAN bus wiring to ABS module.
Test ABS module connector for corrosion.

dtc_codes.description: Lost Communication with Body Control Module
diagnostic_procedure: Check BCM power supply and CAN wiring.
Inspect BCM connector for water ingress.

dtc_codes.description: Lost Communication with Instrument Panel Cluster
diagnostic_procedure: Check IPC power supply and CAN bus connection.
Inspect IPC connector.

dtc_codes.description: Invalid Data Received from ECM/PCM
diagnostic_procedure: Check CAN bus integrity.
Look for intermittent connections.
Update ECM software if available.

dtc_codes.description: Invalid Data Received from Body Control Module
diagnostic_procedure: Check BCM software and CAN communication.
Update BCM if applicable.

dtc_codes.description: Replace Hybrid Battery Pack
diagnostic_procedure: Check individual HV battery module voltages.
Identify weak/failed modules.
Replace complete HV battery or individual modules.
Very common in Pakistan with 10+ year old Prius/Aqua imports – most common hybrid code.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Toyota Prius (Gen 2/3), Aqua/Prius C – very common in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Drive Motor 'A' Performance
diagnostic_procedure: Check MG2 traction motor wiring and inverter.
Scan hybrid system with Toyota Techstream.
Check for coolant leak in inverter.

dtc_codes.description: Hybrid Battery Pack Cooling Fan Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Clean or replace HV battery cooling fan.
Check fan duct for blockage.
Common in Pakistan due to dust clogging the fan.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Toyota Prius Gen 2/3, Aqua – very common in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: Poor Engine Power (Hybrid System)
diagnostic_procedure: Check hybrid battery state of charge.
Inspect MG1/MG2 inverter for fault codes.
Check 12V auxiliary battery condition.

dtc_codes.description: Startability Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel system pressure.
Check crankshaft position sensor.
Verify immobilizer is not active.

dtc_codes.description: Park/Neutral Position Switch Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check PNP switch adjustment and wiring.
Test switch continuity in each position.

dtc_codes.description: VTEC System Malfunction Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check oil pressure (VTEC needs minimum 40 PSI).
Inspect VTEC spool valve for sticking.
Change engine oil if long overdue – very common in Pakistan.
Check oil pressure switch wiring.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Honda Civic 1.8 R18A, City 1.5, BR-V – very common in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: VTEC System Malfunction – Rocker Arm Oil Pressure Switch
diagnostic_procedure: Check engine oil level and pressure.
Inspect VTEC solenoid filter for clogging.
Test VTEC solenoid resistance and operation.

dtc_codes.description: EVAP System Leak – Fuel Tank Side
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel cap seal.
Inspect fuel tank EVAP lines.
Perform EVAP leak test.

dtc_codes.description: Knock Sensor Detection System (KSDS) – Engine Bearing Wear CRITICAL
diagnostic_procedure: STOP ENGINE IMMEDIATELY – this code indicates severe engine bearing wear.
Check rod/main bearing condition.
Perform oil pressure test.
Engine rebuild required if bearings worn.
Common with neglected oil changes.
Hyundai Theta II engine recall-related code.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Hyundai Tucson (Theta II engine), Sonata 2.0/2.4 – SERIOUS FAULT

dtc_codes.description: AGS (Automated Manual Transmission) Clutch Actuator Fault
diagnostic_procedure: Check AGS clutch actuator motor and wiring.
Perform AGS calibration procedure with scan tool.
Inspect AGS clutch engagement.
Note: AGS faults are very common in Pakistan due to high traffic and frequent stop-start driving.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Suzuki Alto AGS, Cultus AGS, WagonR AGS – very common in Pakistan

dtc_codes.description: AGS Gear Shift Actuator Fault
diagnostic_procedure: Check AGS gear shift actuator motor and position sensor.
Perform AGS calibration.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Suzuki Alto AGS, Cultus AGS, WagonR AGS

dtc_codes.description: AGS Clutch Not Engaging Properly
diagnostic_procedure: Check AGS clutch wear.
Perform AGS clutch point learning procedure.
Inspect hydraulic actuator.

dtc_codes.description: AGS Gear Position Sensor Fault
diagnostic_procedure: Check AGS gear position sensor wiring and output in each gear.

dtc_codes.description: ISC Valve Circuit Malfunction (KF Engine)
diagnostic_procedure: Clean throttle body and ISC passages.
Check ISC valve resistance.
Perform throttle body adaptation.

dtc_codes.description: Torque Converter Clutch Stuck Off (D-CVT) – JDM Import
diagnostic_procedure: Change CVT fluid – Pakistani importers rarely change CVT fluid on JDM imports.
Check CVT fluid level and condition.
Inspect torque converter operation.

dtc_codes.description: Engine Over-Temperature Condition
diagnostic_procedure: Check coolant level and for leaks.
Test thermostat operation.
Inspect cooling fan operation.
Critical in Pakistan summers – check radiator for blockage.

dtc_codes.description: CAN Bus Communication Error (Changan Proprietary)
diagnostic_procedure: Check all module CAN connections.
Verify battery voltage is above 11V.
Inspect CAN bus wiring harness.

dtc_codes.description: Internal Control Module EEPROM Error
diagnostic_procedure: Reflash ECM with latest software.
Replace ECM if fault persists after reflash.
dtc_codes.pak_vehicles: Changan Alsvin, Oshan X7, MG HS/ZS (common on Chinese brand ECMs in Pakistan)
